title: Classes should not be overly coupled with other classes
description: In accordance with the Single Responsibility Principle, a class should only have one responsibility. If a class has more than one responsibility, then the responsibilities become coupled. Changes to one responsibility may impair or inhibit the class' ability to meet the others. This leads to fragile designs that break in unpredictable ways when altered. Classes that rely on many other classes tend to aggregate too many responsibilities and should be split into several smaller ones. Nested class dependencies are not considered dependencies of the outer class.
recommendation: Split classes that are coupled with too many other classes into smaller, singularly responsible classes.
impact: Reducing class coupling improves code maintainability and decreases the likelihood of unexpected behavior due to changes in dependent classes.

noncompliant: class Foo { T1 a1; T2 a2; T3 a3; public T4 compute(T5 a, T6 b) { T7 result = a.getResult(b); return result; } public static class Bar { T8 a8; T9 a9; } }
compliant: class Bar { T8 a8; T9 a9; }

checks.message: Classes should not be coupled to too many other classes (more than 5 dependencies detected)
